revisionRequest.description: Add TestimonialsCarousel instance with BOTH loopCarousel AND autoplayCarousel enabled
revisionRequest.rationale: Testing the combination of loop mode with autoplay is critical because it exercises multiple complex accessibility features simultaneously: rotation control button, aria-live management, focus-based pause, and inert attribute handling—all while Swiper is creating duplicate slides for infinite loop.

blockAdded.position: After 'Loop Mode' demo, before 'Minimal' demo (5th demo container)
blockAdded.demoContainerDescription: Tests combined loop and autoplay behavior with rotation control button, aria-live management, and focus-based pause in infinite loop mode.

blockAttributes.heading: Auto-Rotating Infinite Loop
blockAttributes.description: This carousel combines infinite looping with automatic rotation.

testableFeatures: Rotation control button (pause/play toggle) in loop mode
aria-live management: 'off' when auto-rotating, 'polite' when paused
Focus-based autoplay pause: pauses on focusin, resumes on focusout (if user hasn't explicitly stopped)
prefers-reduced-motion: autoplay should be disabled entirely for users with reduced motion preference
Infinite loop behavior with automatic rotation
Interaction between manual navigation (nav buttons) and autoplay in loop mode
Progress bar behavior during autoplay in loop mode
Keyboard navigation with both loop and autoplay active

implementationNotes: Added a new demo-container titled 'Loop + Autoplay' with descriptive explanation of the combined feature test
Positioned after the 'Loop Mode' demo to group loop-related variations together logically
Used postsLimit: 6 (same as loop-only demo) for consistency
Used autoplayDelay: 5 seconds (same as the 'Default Automatic' autoplay demo) for consistency
Enabled both showPagination and showNavigation as requested for full feature testing
This combination exercises the most complex accessibility scenario: rotation control + aria-live + focus management + loop duplicates

rotationControl.description: The carousel-autoplay-toggle button should appear as the first interactive element in the controls bar
rotationControl.ariaLabel: Initially 'Stop slide rotation', changes to 'Start slide rotation' when clicked
rotationControl.icon: Initially pause icon, swaps to play icon when stopped
rotationControl.behavior: Clicking stops/starts autoplay, updates aria-label and icon

ariaLive.description: The .swiper-wrapper element has dynamic aria-live attribute management
ariaLive.pausedState: aria-live='polite' when autoplay is paused (via button or focus)

focusBasedPause.description: Autoplay pauses when any carousel element receives keyboard focus
focusBasedPause.implementation: focusin event handler on .swiper-parent pauses autoplay
focusBasedPause.resumeBehavior: focusout event handler resumes autoplay ONLY if focus leaves the carousel AND user has not explicitly clicked stop button

prefersReducedMotion.description: Autoplay is completely disabled if user has prefers-reduced-motion enabled
prefersReducedMotion.implementation: JavaScript checks window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches and sets autoplayEnabled = false if true
prefersReducedMotion.note: This is checked at page load, so users with reduced motion preference will never see autoplay initialize

loopMode.description: Infinite loop mode creates duplicate slides for seamless transitions
loopMode.inertManagement: The inert attribute management is intentionally SKIPPED when loop is enabled (line 106: 'if ( ! ( element.dataset.loop === '1' ) )')
loopMode.rationale: Swiper creates duplicate slides in loop mode, and managing inert on duplicates would be complex and could interfere with Swiper's internal loop logic

steps: Load the test page and scroll to the 'Loop + Autoplay' demo
Wait for the carousel to auto-rotate to the last slide
Observe the transition from the last slide back to the first slide (should be seamless due to loop mode)
Verify the rotation control button is visible and labeled 'Stop slide rotation'
testingScenarios.expectedResult: Carousel auto-rotates every 5 seconds, smoothly loops from last to first slide without jumping

steps: Click the rotation control button (pause icon)
Observe the icon changes to play icon and aria-label updates to 'Start slide rotation'
Verify autoplay has stopped
Click the button again to resume
Verify autoplay resumes and button returns to pause state
testingScenarios.expectedResult: Button toggles autoplay on/off, icon and aria-label update correctly

steps: Let the carousel auto-rotate
Tab into the carousel (focus on navigation button or video button inside a card)
Verify autoplay pauses immediately when focus enters
Tab out of the carousel entirely
Verify autoplay resumes (if user has not clicked stop button)
testingScenarios.expectedResult: Autoplay pauses on focus, resumes when focus leaves carousel

steps: Click the rotation control button to stop autoplay
Tab into the carousel
Tab out of the carousel
Verify autoplay does NOT resume (because user explicitly stopped it)
testingScenarios.expectedResult: Once user clicks stop, focus-based resume does not re-enable autoplay

steps: Use browser DevTools to inspect the .swiper-wrapper element
Observe aria-live='off' when autoplay is running
Click the pause button
Observe aria-live changes to 'polite'
Click play button
Observe aria-live returns to 'off'
testingScenarios.expectedResult: aria-live attribute updates correctly based on autoplay state

steps: Let the carousel auto-rotate
Click the 'Next' navigation button
Verify autoplay continues after manual navigation (disableOnInteraction: false)
Verify the rotation timer resets after manual navigation
testingScenarios.expectedResult: Manual navigation does not permanently disable autoplay

autoplayConfig.config: autoplay: { delay: 5000, pauseOnMouseEnter: true, disableOnInteraction: false }

rotationControl.description: Implements the pause/play toggle button with icon swapping, aria-label updates, and aria-live management

inertAttributeManagement.note: This code block is SKIPPED when loop mode is enabled (line 106 condition: if ( ! ( element.dataset.loop === '1' ) ))

notes: No PHP/JS/TSX files were modified—only WordPress page content was updated
The 'Loop + Autoplay' demo is now the 5th demo on the test page, positioned logically after the 'Loop Mode' demo
This combination tests the most complex accessibility scenario in the carousel
The existing JavaScript code handles this combination correctly with the implemented accessibility patterns
Test coverage is now complete: autoplay-only (demo 1), loop-only (demo 4), and loop+autoplay (demo 5)
